extracted_information: Falco is a cloud-native security tool designed for real-time runtime security monitoring and threat detection across hosts, containers, Kubernetes, and cloud environments. It uses custom rules based on Linux kernel events and other data sources via plugins, enriching event data with contextual metadata to provide real-time alerts. Falco detects abnormal behavior, potential security threats, and compliance violations.

technology_stack: eBPF (Extended Berkeley Packet Filter) for monitoring system activity

features.name: Cloud Native Threat Detection
features.description: Detects threats across containers, Kubernetes, hosts, and cloud services. Utilizes eBPF, integrates with Kubernetes, and uses plugins to monitor cloud services (e.g., GitHub, Okta, AWS Cloudtrail). Addresses malicious behavior at scale.

features.name: Real Time Detection and Monitoring
features.description: Provides streaming detection of unexpected behavior, configuration changes, and attacks. Acts as a fundamental layer of defense against security blind spots and zero-day bugs. The streaming approach enables real-time response while minimizing storage costs and complexity. Comes with ready-to-use, customizable rules.

features.name: Security Event Monitoring & Alerting
features.description: Leverages custom rules on Linux kernel events and other data sources via plugins. Enriches event data with contextual metadata to deliver real-time alerts. Alerts are in JSON format, facilitating storage, analysis, and triggering reactions.

features.name: Integration with 50+ Systems
features.description: Falco alerts can be forwarded to over 50 third-party SIEM and data lake systems for analysis, storage, or reaction.

features.name: Open Source
features.description: A multi-vendor and widely adopted solution, created cloud-native within the same community as Kubernetes, Prometheus, and OPA. Easy to audit, extend, and integrate.

features.name: Regulatory Compliance
features.description: Helps maintain compliance in cloud-native systems through intelligent monitoring and rule-based detection.
